name: West: Royal Lamb Chops Braised with Nuts and Saffron
recipeIngredient: 1 Fresh hot green chile, finely chopped
1/2 teaspoon Royal cumin (or regular cumin) seeds
3 Lamb rib chops, 4 ounces each, trimmed of all fat
1/2 teaspoon Saffron threads, dissolved in 2 tablespoons hot water
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 2 tablespoons of the butter in a heavy pan over medium-high heat. Add almonds and pistachios; cook, stirring, until light brown, about 4 minutes. Remove with a slotted spoon and set aside.
2. Combine garlic, ginger, coriander seeds, cardamom pods, chili, peppercorns, cloves, cumin seeds, mace, and water in a blender. Blend to form a smooth paste.
3. Arrange lamb chops in a large shallow dish. Rub the spice mixture over both sides of each chop. Cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
4. Heat remaining 2 tablespoons butter in a large, heavy skillet over medium-high heat. Add lamb and brown well on both sides. Add 1/4 cup water and bring to a boil. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer until lamb is tender, about 10 minutes.
5. Add the saffron-water mixture; stir gently. Cover and cook until lamb is fully cooked and tender, about 10 more minutes. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt if needed.
6. Stir in peas and cook for an additional 5 minutes until sauce thickens.
7. Transfer lamb to a heated platter. Pour the sauce over the chops. Top with fried nuts and fresh cilantro.
Note: Royal cumin (black cumin) or shahi jeera gets its name from the royal Mogul dishes it was most used in. It is available at Indian markets.
